mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of instances when the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer spending without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative in >50% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic activity and corporate output, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods where industrial production increases by more than 0.5% month-over-month during recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation raises expectations for aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate earnings and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of occurrences over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal confidence in future economic growth and stimulate construction-related corporate revenues, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of months where housing starts exceed their 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production indicates immediate supply-side weakness and declining corporate output, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >60% of instances when industrial production declines by more than 0.3% month-over-month.
